# Developing with Neo4j MCP Tools

Learn how to use the Neo4j MCP server and tools to create intelligent AI applications

[Learn more about this course](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools)

## Concepts

* **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** — Open protocol (Anthropic) that lets AI agents discover and call external **tools** in a standard way, with **servers** exposing capabilities and **clients** maintaining stateful connections (**stdio** or **HTTP** with SSE/streaming).
* **Agent** — System that acts toward a goal using tool calls; often structured with the **ReAct** loop: **planning** (break down tasks, pick tools), **reasoning** (choose tools from descriptions), **acting** (run tools, feed results back).
* **MCP host** — Application (Claude Desktop, VS Code, Cursor, or your own app) that holds session context, manages clients, and decides **which** tool to run with **which** arguments.
* **MCP server** — Process that registers **tools** (id, description, parameters), optional **resources** (read-only URIs), and **prompt templates**; can be local or remote.
* **Neo4j MCP server (`neo4j-mcp`)** — MCP server that exposes Neo4j-focused tools so agents can inspect schema and run Cypher **read** and **write** paths under your host’s policies.
* **`get-schema`** — Tool with **no inputs**; returns JSON describing labels, properties, and relationship patterns so the model does not invent labels or relationship types.
* **`read-cypher`** — Runs **read-only** Cypher inside a read transaction; rejects writes, admin/schema operations, and `PROFILE` (per course); follows the DB **routing** policy in clusters.
* **`write-cypher`** — Runs write-capable Cypher (`CREATE`, `MERGE`, `DELETE`, `SET`, …); can be disabled for the process with **`NEO4J_READ_ONLY=true`**.
* **`list-gds-procedures`** — Lists **Graph Data Science** procedures available on the instance so the agent knows what GDS calls exist.
* **Configuration** — Host-specific JSON (e.g. `.claude_desktop_config.json`, `.vscode/mcp.json`) naming the server, **command** + **`args`** (not one concatenated shell string), and **`env`** or **`envFile`** for secrets and connection details.
* **Environment variables** — Typically `NEO4J_URI`, `NEO4J_USERNAME`, `NEO4J_PASSWORD`, `NEO4J_DATABASE`; optional `NEO4J_NAMESPACE` when running **multiple** server entries to prefix tool names.

---

## Installation and host configuration

Install the `neo4j-mcp` binary from the Neo4j MCP distribution, put it on your **PATH**, then register it in your host. Use separate **`command`** and **`args`** arrays (do not fold flags into a single executable string).

**Claude Desktop-style configuration** (illustrative; adjust paths and secrets):

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "neo4j-mcp": {
      "command": "path/to/neo4j-mcp",
      "args": [
        "--neo4j-read-only",
        "true",
        "--neo4j-transport-mode",
        "stdio"
      ],
      "env": {
        "NEO4J_URI": "bolt://localhost:7687",
        "NEO4J_USERNAME": "neo4j",
        "NEO4J_PASSWORD": "<your-password>",
        "NEO4J_DATABASE": "neo4j"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

**VS Code** commonly uses `type: "stdio"`, the `neo4j-mcp` command, and an **`envFile`** pointing at a workspace `.env` so secrets stay out of JSON:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "neo4j": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "neo4j-mcp",
      "envFile": "${workspaceFolder}/.env"
    }
  }
}
```

**`.env` for Neo4j** (placeholders match what you use in Aura or self-managed):

```bash
# bolt:// or neo4j:// / neo4j+s:// depending on deployment
NEO4J_URI="bolt://localhost:7687"
NEO4J_USERNAME="neo4j"
NEO4J_PASSWORD="your-password"
NEO4J_DATABASE="neo4j"
```

[Reference: Agentic Systems and MCP > Installing an MCP server](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools/1-what-is-mcp/3-installing-servers)

[Reference: Agentic Systems and MCP > Installing the Neo4j MCP server](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools/1-what-is-mcp/4c-install-mcp-neo4j-cypher)

---

## Neo4j MCP tools (behavior and safety)

| Tool | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `get-schema` | Labels, relationship types, property keys — context for correct Cypher |
| `read-cypher` | Read-only queries; no writes; rejects `PROFILE` and admin/schema-style use (per course) |
| `write-cypher` | Mutating Cypher; honor approvals; disable with `NEO4J_READ_ONLY=true` if needed |
| `list-gds-procedures` | Expose which GDS procedures exist on the server |

**Operational notes**

* In **VS Code**, start the server from the Command Palette (**MCP: List servers** → start your Neo4j entry). Tools are available when the chat is in **Agent** mode.
* Approve tool access deliberately; **`read-cypher`** is constrained to read transactions so routine approval is lower risk than **`write-cypher`**.

[Reference: Using Neo4j MCP Tools > Using the Neo4j Cypher MCP Server](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools/2-using-neo4j-mcp-tools/1-mcp-neo4j-cypher)

---

## Schema inspection (`get-schema`) and example JSON shape

The agent calls **`get-schema`** (no parameters). The payload describes labels, properties (with type hints), and outgoing relationship patterns. Example structure (Movies-style graph):

```json
[
  {
    "label": "Movie",
    "attributes": {
      "title": "STRING indexed",
      "imdbRating": "FLOAT indexed",
      "revenue": "INTEGER"
    },
    "relationships": {
      "IN_GENRE": "Genre"
    }
  },
  {
    "label": "User",
    "attributes": {
      "userId": "STRING unique indexed",
      "name": "STRING indexed"
    },
    "relationships": {
      "RATED": "Movie"
    }
  }
]
```

[Reference: Using Neo4j MCP Tools > Using the Neo4j Cypher MCP Server](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools/2-using-neo4j-mcp-tools/1-mcp-neo4j-cypher)

[Reference: Using Neo4j MCP Tools > Reading the Graph Schema](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools/2-using-neo4j-mcp-tools/2c-reading-graph-schema)

---

## Read path: `read-cypher` patterns

Ask natural-language questions; the agent should emit parameterized Cypher. Examples from the course:

**Top movies by revenue**

```cypher
MATCH (m:Movie)
WHERE m.revenue IS NOT NULL
RETURN m.title AS title, m.revenue AS revenue
ORDER BY m.revenue DESC
LIMIT 10
```

**Highest IMDB rating** — filter out null ratings so ordering is meaningful:

```cypher
MATCH (m:Movie)
WHERE m.imdbRating IS NOT NULL
RETURN m.title AS title, m.imdbRating AS rating
ORDER BY m.imdbRating DESC
LIMIT 1
```

[Reference: Using Neo4j MCP Tools > Generating Cypher Statements](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools/2-using-neo4j-mcp-tools/3c-generating-cypher-statements)

---

## Write path: `write-cypher` with prior reads

Agents often **`read-cypher`** first to disambiguate titles or ids, then **`write-cypher`**. Example pattern (conceptual): rate a movie for a user — match user and movie, `MERGE` the relationship, `SET` properties:

```cypher
MATCH (u:User { userId: $userId })
MATCH (m:Movie { title: $title })
MERGE (u)-[r:RATED]->(m)
SET r.rating = $rating,
    r.timestamp = toInteger(datetime().epochSeconds)
RETURN u.name AS user, m.title AS movie, r.rating AS rating, r.timestamp AS timestamp
```

Pass parameters from the agent/tool layer as JSON alongside the query string when your integration supports it.

[Reference: Using Neo4j MCP Tools > Using the Neo4j Cypher MCP Server](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools/2-using-neo4j-mcp-tools/1-mcp-neo4j-cypher)

---

## Python application: driver + validated Cypher (GraphRAG-style workflow)

Install the driver:

```bash
pip install neo4j
```

The course challenge asks for a CLI that takes a **genre** and returns the **top 5** movies in that genre by **`imdbRating`**, using **non-null** ratings, after **schema understanding** and **query validation** with MCP tools.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# Neo4j Python driver 5.x — CLI: top 5 movies in a genre by imdbRating (non-null)

import os
import sys
from neo4j import GraphDatabase


def get_top_movies_by_genre(genre_name: str) -> list[dict]:
    # Cypher aligned with a Movie–Genre model: (Genre)<-[:IN_GENRE]-(Movie)
    query = """
    MATCH (g:Genre)<-[:IN_GENRE]-(m:Movie)
    WHERE g.name = $genre_name AND m.imdbRating IS NOT NULL
    RETURN m.title AS title, m.imdbRating AS imdbRating
    ORDER BY m.imdbRating DESC
    LIMIT 5
    """

    neo4j_uri = os.getenv("NEO4J_URI")
    neo4j_username = os.getenv("NEO4J_USERNAME")
    neo4j_password = os.getenv("NEO4J_PASSWORD")
    database = os.getenv("NEO4J_DATABASE", "neo4j")

    if not all([neo4j_uri, neo4j_username, neo4j_password]):
        raise RuntimeError("Set NEO4J_URI, NEO4J_USERNAME, and NEO4J_PASSWORD")

    with GraphDatabase.driver(neo4j_uri, auth=(neo4j_username, neo4j_password)) as driver:
        driver.verify_connectivity()
        eager = driver.execute_query(
            query,
            {"genre_name": genre_name},
            database_=database,
        )
        return [r.data() for r in eager.records]


def main() -> None:
    genre_name = input("Enter a genre name: ").strip()
    if not genre_name:
        print("Error: Genre name cannot be empty", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    try:
        rows = get_top_movies_by_genre(genre_name)
        if not rows:
            print(f"No movies found for genre: {genre_name}")
            return
        print(f"\nTop 5 movies in '{genre_name}' by IMDB rating:")
        for i, row in enumerate(rows, 1):
            print(f"{i}. {row['title']} — {row['imdbRating']}")
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"Error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

**Agent prompt pattern (from the course)** — forces schema use and **read-cypher** validation before coding:

```text
Create a simple command line application in python that:

1. Connects to Neo4j using the Neo4j Python driver using environment variables: NEO4J_URI, NEO4J_USERNAME, NEO4J_PASSWORD, NEO4J_DATABASE.
2. Accepts a Genre name as a text input
3. Runs a Cypher statement that produces a list of the top five movies ordered by their imdbRating in descending order.

Rules:

* The query must filter on non-null values
* You must use tools to understand the schema information
* You must validate your Cypher statement on real data from the database before you write it to the application.
```

[Reference: Using Neo4j MCP Tools > Building applications with MCP tools](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools/2-using-neo4j-mcp-tools/4c-build-application)

---

## Multiple databases in one host

You can register **several** Neo4j MCP server blocks under `mcpServers`, each with its own `env` and optional **`NEO4J_NAMESPACE`** so tool names are prefixed and disambiguated:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "movies-neo4j": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "neo4j-mcp",
      "env": {
        "NEO4J_URI": "neo4j+s://demo.neo4jlabs.com",
        "NEO4J_USERNAME": "recommendations",
        "NEO4J_PASSWORD": "recommendations",
        "NEO4J_DATABASE": "recommendations"
      }
    },
    "local-neo4j": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "neo4j-mcp",
      "env": {
        "NEO4J_URI": "bolt://localhost:7687",
        "NEO4J_USERNAME": "neo4j",
        "NEO4J_PASSWORD": "password",
        "NEO4J_DATABASE": "neo4j",
        "NEO4J_NAMESPACE": "local"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

[Reference: Agentic Systems and MCP > Installing the Neo4j MCP server](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools/1-what-is-mcp/4c-install-mcp-neo4j-cypher)

---

## Production and security practices

* **Review** MCP server implementations (especially community servers) before production use.
* **Know** what each tool can do; grant **write** capabilities only when needed; prefer **`NEO4J_READ_ONLY=true`** when mutations must be impossible at the server.
* **Test** tool outputs in safe environments; agents interact with **real** databases.
* **Enforce** authentication and authorization at the database and network layers; **monitor** and **audit** tool usage.

[Reference: Using Neo4j MCP Tools > Summary & Next Steps](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools/2-using-neo4j-mcp-tools/5-summary)

---

## MCP fundamentals (quick reference)

**ReAct-style loop:** plan → pick tools → execute → feed results back until the goal is satisfied.

**Roles:** **Server** exposes tools; **Client** holds the connection; **Host** orchestrates which tool runs.

[Reference: Agentic Systems and MCP > What is MCP?](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools/1-what-is-mcp/1-getting-started)

[Reference: Agentic Systems and MCP > MCP Pop Quiz](mdc:https:/graphacademy.neo4j.com/courses/genai-mcp-neo4j-tools/1-what-is-mcp/2-pop-quiz)
